Skill: 2H Blunt Atk Delay: 40
DMG: 30 Dmg Bonus: 142
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WT: 6.8 Size: LARGE
Class: PAL MNK BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

▲ Page top
compare to patchwork jetsam
QuoteReply
# Mar 11 2003 at 5:54 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255037618
231 posts
With the release of LOY this item has become obsolete at least for monks. The Jetsam not only looks cool as hell but is:

30 damage
35 delay
+4 wisdom
+10 save versus fire
and the weight is only 4 stones!!!

Best part is some kindly gentleman had one for sale in the Bazaar today for 75pp. I would have payed many times that amount for it but I'm glad I didn't have to. I wonder if he knew the value of what he had.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 05 2003 at 8:41 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) The weight/dmg/dly raitio sucks. To compare it with that wu piece of crap 28/30(?) and ten stones? Yet another piece of crap.
Weight
QuoteReply
# Oct 01 2002 at 6:54 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759769277
12 posts
It is very easy to keep your weight down even with this weapon, I have a 57th monk on Xev that wields a T-staff 29/30 , weight 10.0 and still have his total weight at 19. I carry limited food and water and only the plat that I loot. Secondly weight is not an issue for monks in MOST circumstances, when grouped you are NOT the main tank, you shouldnt be getting hit except for pulls. If you are getting hit there is something wrong with your tactics.
▲ Page top
baton over staff?
QuoteReply
# Jul 18 2002 at 9:23 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759558839
5 posts
baton of faith 30 dmg 40 delay.. compared with staff of battle, 31 dmg 37 delay.. (which i have) i cant see it worth upgrading (or downgrading as it would be?) to a baton of faith.. but id like a pc on my staff i have?

I want one
QuoteReply
# Jun 02 2002 at 7:26 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759157062
11 posts
Hey im a 44 paladin planning on getting one of these. so far i am using an argent defender, and i sorta want one of these instead. but, im out of money except about 140pp atm and my 2hb is kinda low. i am currently using a silver etched warhammer to boost it up... was wondering if an argent defender along with a silver etched warhammer would be a good deal for one of these. If not, im a sad pally =(

I know, i know what your thinking, i know... just because im a pally i automatically stink... and having rodcet nife as a deity just makes it worse. =P

initials?
QuoteReply
# May 15 2002 at 4:52 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591776521220
57 posts
ok someone clarify some of these initials for a noob please. What is a KD? JM? I have a noob Monk and would like to know all my weapon options
. thanks
▲ Page top
RE: initials?
QuoteReply
# Jun 24 2002 at 11:46 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591764595777
92 posts
Also...

CoT = Cane of the Tranquil
WTS, or Wu's = generally reffers to a Wu's Trance Stick

SoS = Staff of Shielding
WQS = Wu's Quivering Staff (rare as hell)
T Staff = Tranquil Staff (rare and uber as hell)
GSC = Goranga Spiked Club (no-drop, from Maidens Eye on the moon)

:) Just a few more initials exposed...

Ashremm
▲ Page top
RE: initials?
QuoteReply
# May 16 2002 at 10:47 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591776161318
12 posts
KD = Knuckle Dusters
JM = Jade Mace
▲ Page top
have one on Ayone Ro
QuoteReply
# May 01 2002 at 3:32 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253254638
2 posts
Send tell if interested to izra
▲ Page top
Mediocre
QuoteReply
# Mar 01 2002 at 6:05 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591766688531
6 posts
I am a level 40 Monk and would not consider down grading to this weapon. At level 40 with 112 str this weapon will do a mediocre 38.87dmg/sec while 2 CoT will do 40.82dmg/sec. - why have dual weild of 252 if you don't use it? For the price, get 2 KD and keep the change and hit for 43.52dmg/sec. Or you can do what I did and get two weapons that proc use the extra pp to get your dex up and proc 4-6 time per fight. If your worried about the taunt then group with tanks that dual weild and use fast weapons that way they will keep the taunt and the mobs will drop quick.
▲ Page top
RE: Mediocre
QuoteReply
# May 14 2002 at 11:01 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591775089716
6 posts
I dont know you but it seems you are not considering the chance of a mob having a damage shield up. I have a 45 monk which 90% of the time I solo with, he dual weilds a Wu Stick and KD (I wear SCHW also), so if a mob has a damage shield on him this means major owies for me (even the green ones). Just now I trained my 2hb from 0 to master to help deal with this problem. The idea is to hit the mob for more, less times, taking less damage from the DS (yes I realize it might be less damage over time, but when you are soloing like I do, hp is at a premium). So as you can see, there are more factors in deciding which weapon(s) to then just what does the most damage over time...

Tygur Uppercut 45 Monk
Tribunal

Edited, Tue May 14 12:07:43 2002
▲ Page top
RE: Mediocre
QuoteReply
# Apr 24 2002 at 12:07 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591770770502
32 posts
What????????? I dont wanna flame ya here but your math is totally off. You say wielding 2 CoT's(Cane of Tranquil, 11Dmg 25Dly) will do 40.82dmg/sec, rofl. damage per sec is calculated (2xDMG)/(DLY/10) which would give you 8.8dmg/sec. However at lvl40 your prim hand damage bonus is 5 so your prim hand dmg/sec
((2xdmg)+dmg_bonus)/(dly/10) with bonus comes to a cool 10.80. Now your thinking well great I can double that damage/sec since I am wielding 2 of them.....WRONG. Your off hand has no damage bonus and even with your dual wield skill maxed at 252 your only dual wielding 48.67% of the time at level40. All said and done your damage/sec with 2 Canes of Tranquil comes to an average, because you will dual wield more or less sometimes, of 15.08dmg/sec. Im not sure the damage bonus of a 40dly weapon at lvl40 but I assume its somewhere around 10 since at lvl55 its 24. This would mean that the Baton of Faith is 17.50dmg/sec, not mediocre at all and in fact better damage/sec then wielding most 2 one handed weapons at any level. For your enjoyment here are a few weapon combos with average damage/sec with prim hand weapon coming first at level40 with dual wield maxed:
Wu Trance/Wu Trance = 14.34
Wu Trance/KD = 15.42
Wu Trance/CoT = 14.84
Wu Trance/SOS = 16.47
SoS/KD = 18.80
CoT/CoT = 15.08
JM/KD = 17.64
JM/SOS = 18.69
KD/KD = 16.65
KD/WU Trance = 15.57
As you can see from the last entry that the slower weapon, KD, in prim and faster,Wu, in secondary does more damage/sec. This is because your damage bonus for your level is not high enough to yet to make up the difference. This however changes as the damage bonus for you prim hand goes up. The following combos are for a level50 where the prim hand bonus is now 8 instead of 5 at level40.
Wu/Wu = 16.14
Wu/KD = 17.26
Wu/CoT = 16.65
Wu/SOS = 18.33
SoS/KD = 20.03
CoT/CoT = 16.43
JM/KD = 19.48
JM/SOS = 20.56
KD/KD = 17.89
KD/Wu = 16.77
As you can see your prim hand is know better off having a faster weapon in it. It's all a matter of what works for you. Sometimes your better off having a faster weapon simply for spell interupts. Other times you just want straight out damage where a slower weapon with higher damage or proc is more usefull. Anyway I hope this helped someone out there.

Btw at level 50 your Dual Wield percentage is also higher at 50.33% compared to that of 48.67% at level 40. Another thing I would like to add is that a Delay of 20 means you swing your weapon once every 2 seconds. Thus the 40.82dmg/sec mentioned above by Pengu with 2 CoT's(11dmg/25dly) would mean that he is constantly hitting for 50+ dmg per swing on each hand, which is just not possible at level40 with two Cane of Tranquils.

Also, at level 55 the Baton of Faith average dmg/sec is 21.00, which is still higher then all listed above except for the JM/SOS combo which is higher at 21.77dmg/sec.

Edited, Wed Apr 24 14:34:52 2002
▲ Page top
RE: Mediocre
QuoteReply
# May 02 2002 at 6:19 AM Rating: Decent
Morigu
Sage
239 posts
Actually, my understanding that your chance to dual wield or double attack are this formula:

DW= (level + DW skill)/400
a level 60 + 252 maxed DW = 312/400 = 78%

DA = (level + DA skill)/500
a level 60 + 250 maxed DA = 310/500 = 62%


Morigu
(and yes, I know it was a level 40 char, 292/400= 73% for his dual wield)
____________________________
Voidslayer Jhereg, Destroyer of Worlds, Monk, <Celestial Navigators>, Drinal server
Vanidor Silverbranch, Wizard, <Celestial Navigators>, Drinal server
▲ Page top
RE: Mediocre
QuoteReply
# May 07 2002 at 10:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770770502
32 posts
Well you could be right, the formula I have is

DW=(lvl+DW skill)/600

It may have been changed some time ago when VI revamped melee damage, my info is based on a spreadsheet from monkly-bussiness which is probably about 2 years old.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Feb 07 2002 at 2:37 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) ignore this post
Not a bad little stick
QuoteReply
# Jan 31 2002 at 8:45 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592341384526
Scholar
18 posts
I recently got one of these in a trade. Tried it out for the first time on a blue con giant in BW. First hit 147. Not too shabby. Over time I watched the damage. No hit less than 41 and the max I saw was 160. I did have a STR buff on me so my strength was about 170 at the time. Trashed even the named giant quite rapidly.

This is as good as the Light Velium Brawl stick, apart from its weight which is 6.8 as opposed to 3.6.

Im a level 52 monk. I can get my weight to 18 or at worst 20 when wielding this stick, even with my bags full of my resist gear.

Yes, I WOULD rather have a T Staff, but I think it will be a while before I see one.

Fine weapon for a monk or a Paladin.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 13 2002 at 12:49 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I want to buy one of these on thet Cazic-Thule server. Send me a tell if you are selling one.
useless hell
QuoteReply
# Nov 20 2001 at 7:33 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763858409
34 posts
To the person who said this is usless to a monk, hope you dont play a monk) next to Tstaff or some unobtainable veshann sleepers items) this staff rocks for a monk.
1) hasted u r doing ALOT of damage
2) at the lvl u can get this staff, 10 wt dosent mean anything, u can take the AC loss
3) ill explain 2. What does AC matter if your noit getting hit? And u wont as much with this weapon..as opposed 2 duel wielding faster weapon, fast weapons= aggro, why tanks swing lammy
4) usually when pullin, u will FD at tank or tag to get aggro off of you, just put it up pulling, then switch back) u keep AC on pulls, but get to be a walking DoT after)
P.S. yes Tstaff rocks, is better, but how many have u seen drop?) and forget sleepers unless your guild is quite uber. So no primal/priceles for me and im not payin 60k for a staff)

Tumtuk Kyckabut
46 Monk
Mith Marr

This weapon is a good weapon indeed but you guys should check out the Velium Brawl Stick. 30/40 only weighs 3.6! Monks/War and casters and Pal and so forth can use it. If you ever see one of these, try to get it before you get a Baton Of Faith. 3.6 Weight it incredible for 2hb.

This weapon is a good weapon indeed but you guys should check out the Velium Brawl Stick. 30/40 only weighs 3.6! Monks/War and casters and Pal and so forth can use it. If you ever see one of these, try to get it before you get a Baton Of Faith. 3.6 Weight it incredible for 2hb.

Just some observations . . .
QuoteReply
# Jul 18 2001 at 8:20 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I have one of these and have been using it to increase my skill at 2HB. As a monk at level 51, I've noticed I can hit my targets for a max of 120 with a skill in the 190's.
One thing I have noticed about this staff is that because it does NOT proc anything, the majority of the time the aggro stays on the main tank where it belongs. Less aggro = less damage to my HP = happy cleric with one less person to heal. Just because a weapon procs does not necessarily make it a good thing in some situations. Just an observation.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 12 2001 at 4:08 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I've got one of these. I'm level 29 and i hit dual/triple 62 sometimes. Oh yeah, and even with it only weigh 12 pounds :P...
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 14 2001 at 1:29 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) ummm, you can't triple hit with a 2H item...there is no such skill as triple attack. and at 29 you can't possibly mean that you sometimes double attack and riposte in the same round. sorry buddy, no triples with 2HB
RE: Is a Good Monk Weap :P
QuoteReply
# Jun 22 2001 at 8:08 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248280564
4 posts
You are correct that there is no skill called triple attack. However a little research on this topic you'll find that anyone with double attack has chance of triple or quad attacking. I forget where I found the chart or I'd provide a link , but for example say at your level you have a 52% chance of double attack, triple might be around 2.3% and quad like .7%. These numbers aren't accurate just an idea of how it works. If anyone knows where this chart is please post a link here I'd like to see it again.
▲ Page top
RE: Is a Good Monk Weap :P
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2001 at 12:05 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757704817
Scholar
22 posts
Hehehe... You forget that it is based on the DUAL WIELD + DOUBLE ATTACK...

if you have dual wield you can attack with either hand...
if you have double attack you can attack twice PER HAND...
if you have a 2h weapon you are using ONE weapon and therefore NOT using dual wield (it is blatantly obvious, but some need to be reminded that if you are using 1 weapon, you are not using 2)... which means double attack is the only skilled bein' used... a Double attack is the ONLY thing you can do.

Now with a riposte it can seem like you are getting a triple... but thats all...

Double attack does not dictate whether you get triple or quads... double attack AND dual wield combined dictates that.
▲ Page top
RE: Is a Good Monk Weap :P
QuoteReply

__DEL__1591756095442
